items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: ‘I want to get that trophy’: Wembanyama talks motivation after Game 1
items.blurb: ‘I want to get that trophy’: Wembanyama talks motivation after Game 1 — San Antonio Spurs superstar Victor Wembanyama talks with the media after his 41-point and 24-rebound performance in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals and discusses his motivation for Game 1 and the thought of him being the NBA’s best player.

items.headline: Cavaliers owner Dan Gilbert creates a home-court advantage on the road
items.blurb: Cavaliers owner Dan Gilbert creates a home-court advantage on the road — The Cleveland Cavaliers won two road games in Detroit to advance to the Eastern Conference Finals. Their owner made sure the Cavs got to see some friendly faces in the Motor City.

items.headline: Cavaliers-Knicks ECF preview: Five keys that will decide who advances to NBA Finals
items.blurb: Cavaliers-Knicks ECF preview: Five keys that will decide who advances to NBA Finals — The New York Knicks found out who their Eastern Conference Finals opponent is on Sunday when the Cleveland Cavaliers blew out the Detroit Pistons in Game 7 of the East semifinals.

items.headline: Spurs' Victor Wembanyama draws attention with pregame routine
items.blurb: Spurs' Victor Wembanyama draws attention with pregame routine — Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs duked it out against the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals on Monday, and while the Thunder are largely expected to take this series, by the looks of the first game, it won't be easy.

items.headline: Spurs’ Wembanyama, Vassell turn up defence late to seal Game 1 on the road
items.blurb: Spurs’ Wembanyama, Vassell turn up defence late to seal Game 1 on the road — The San Antonio Spurs’ Victor Wembanyama bothers a shot, Devin Vassell comes up with the block, and Wembanyama blocks a shot late in Game 1 to help the Spurs defeat the Oklahoma City Thunder in double-overtime 122-115 for a 1-0 series lead.

items.headline: ‘Video game stats’: Basketball fans react to Wembanyama’s Game 1 heroics
items.blurb: ‘Video game stats’: Basketball fans react to Wembanyama’s Game 1 heroics — If there weren’t already plenty of reasons to marvel at Victor Wembanyama’s brilliance, the French phenom gave fans some more.

items.headline: James Harden's trade demand pays off with return to conference finals
items.blurb: James Harden's trade demand pays off with return to conference finals — Midway through the 2025-26 season, James Harden learned that the Los Angeles Clippers weren't offering him a contract extension and worked his way to the Cleveland Cavaliers. Now he's back in the conference finals for the first time in eight seasons.

items.headline: Are Pistons still confident in HC J.B. Bickerstaff after playoff exit?
items.blurb: Are Pistons still confident in HC J.B. Bickerstaff after playoff exit? — Head coach J.B. Bickerstaff and the Detroit Pistons are officially out of the postseason after falling to the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference semifinals in Detroit on Sunday, 125-94.

items.headline: The 'NBA steals-per-game leaders' quiz
items.blurb: The 'NBA steals-per-game leaders' quiz — How many of the NBA players to lead the league in steals per game since the merger (1976-77) can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: Spurs’ Wembanyama delivers and-one dunk over Holmgren in Game 1 thriller
items.blurb: Spurs’ Wembanyama delivers and-one dunk over Holmgren in Game 1 thriller — In double overtime, with Spurs leading 115-114, Wembanyama powered through Holmgren for a huge and-one dunk, extending the lead to 118-114 in Game 1 against Thunder.

items.headline: Wembanyama takes aim from logo range with clutch 3, sends Game 1 to 2OT
items.blurb: Wembanyama takes aim from logo range with clutch 3, sends Game 1 to 2OT — The San Antonio Spurs’ Victor Wembanyama gets the transition pass from Stephon Castle, sets his feet, and nails the long three-pointer to tie Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals at 108 and send it into overtime. The Spurs won the game 122-115.

items.headline: Wembanyama powers Spurs to 2OT victory over Thunder in Game 1 of WCF
items.blurb: Wembanyama powers Spurs to 2OT victory over Thunder in Game 1 of WCF

items.headline: Spurs' Victor Wembanyama outduels Alex Caruso in Game 1 win
items.blurb: Spurs' Victor Wembanyama outduels Alex Caruso in Game 1 win — The Western Conference Finals were shaping up to be a battle between two-time NBA MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Defensive Player of the Year Victor Wembanyama.

items.headline: Holmgren denies Wembanyama at the buzzer to force overtime in Game 1
items.blurb: Holmgren denies Wembanyama at the buzzer to force overtime in Game 1 — With three seconds left and the game tied 101-101, Chet Holmgren came up with a massive block on Victor Wembanyama’s potential game-winner to send Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals into overtime.

items.headline: Joe Ingles Signs Two-Year Deal With Melbourne United
items.blurb: Joe Ingles Signs Two-Year Deal With Melbourne United — Joe Ingles has confirmed he will sign with Melbourne United on a two-year deal. Ingles began his career in 2006 with the South Dragons and won an NBL championship with the club. After leaving Australia in 2009, Ingles spent three seasons playing in Spain for Granada and Barcelona before joining Maccabi Tel Aviv for the 2013-14 season. Ingles t

items.headline: Spurs’ Wembanyama throws down sweet left-handed slam in the paint
items.blurb: Spurs’ Wembanyama throws down sweet left-handed slam in the paint — San Antonio Spurs superstar Victor Wembanyama sets a screen for Stephon Castle, rolls into the lane, catches the pass from Castle, and quickly turns in one motion to finish with a left-handed dunk on Chet Holmgren and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander receives MVP trophy from NBA Commissioner Adam Silver
items.blurb: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander receives MVP trophy from NBA Commissioner Adam Silver

items.headline: Commissioner Silver presents Gilgeous-Alexander with 2025-26 MVP trophy
items.blurb: Commissioner Silver presents Gilgeous-Alexander with 2025-26 MVP trophy — Watch as NBA commissioner Adam Silver presents the Oklahoma City Thunder’s Shai Gilgeous-Alexander with his second consecutive Most Valuable Player award, prior to Game 1. The Canadian is the 14th player in NBA history to win back-to-back MVP awards.

items.headline: Darius Acuff Jr. Strongly Linked With Kings In Draft
items.blurb: Darius Acuff Jr. Strongly Linked With Kings In Draft — Darius Acuff Jr. has been strongly linked with the Sacramento Kings, who own the seventh overall pick. The Kings have a need at point guard and Acuff would give them a dynamic option at the position after trading away Tyrese Haliburton in 2022 and De'Aaron Fox in 2025. Acuff's father played for Kings' general manager Scott Perry at Eastern Ken

items.headline: De'Aaron Fox Out For Game 1 Of Western Conference Finals With Ankle Injury
items.blurb: De'Aaron Fox Out For Game 1 Of Western Conference Finals With Ankle Injury — De'Aaron Fox has been ruled out by the San Antonio Spurs for Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals due to a right ankle sprain. Fox has been dealing with the ankle injury since Game 4 of the second round when Ayo Dosunmu landed on his ankle. Fox finished Game 4 and subsequently played in Games 5 and 6. Fox is averaging

items.headline: Spurs’ De’Aaron Fox to miss Game 1 vs. Thunder due to ankle injury
items.blurb: Spurs’ De’Aaron Fox to miss Game 1 vs. Thunder due to ankle injury — The San Antonio Spurs will be without their starting point guard to open the Western Conference Finals.

items.headline: Knicks get major OG Anunoby update before Game 1 of ECF
items.blurb: Knicks get major OG Anunoby update before Game 1 of ECF — Jalen Brunson and the New York Knicks are coming up on Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals against the Cleveland Cavaliers on Tuesday.

items.headline: Patrick Ewing gives Knicks advice for Eastern Conference Finals matchup with Cavaliers
items.blurb: Patrick Ewing gives Knicks advice for Eastern Conference Finals matchup with Cavaliers — Ewing was asked what he would say if he were given an opportunity to address the Knicks ahead of the matchup versus the Cavaliers.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander continues Hall of Fame path with second straight MVP award
items.blurb: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander continues Hall of Fame path with second straight MVP award — The Oklahoma City Thunder are halfway to an NBA championship repeat. Their best player, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, has already earned his own individual repeat by winning the league's MVP award for the second year in a row.

items.headline: Cavaliers have reportedly already made decision on James Harden
items.blurb: Cavaliers have reportedly already made decision on James Harden — The Cleveland Cavaliers do not yet know how their season will end, but they appear ready to commit to Harden.

items.headline: Pistons learned a lesson in tough Game 7 loss to Cavaliers
items.blurb: Pistons learned a lesson in tough Game 7 loss to Cavaliers — The Detroit Pistons had high hopes heading into Game 7 of their Eastern Conference semifinal series against the Cleveland Cavaliers, but things went the other way as the team fell hard, losing 125-94.

items.headline: NBA Eastern Conference Finals preview: Now or never for Knicks, Cavaliers
items.blurb: NBA Eastern Conference Finals preview: Now or never for Knicks, Cavaliers — The Conference Finals always comes with heavy pressure, such is the nature of the beast. But there’s an added element for the Cavaliers and Knicks, both of whom may be nearing the end of their runway for contention. Zulfi Sheikh previews the action.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander vs. Victor Wembanyama makes for historic WCF
items.blurb: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander vs. Victor Wembanyama makes for historic WCF — Expected to be a classic battle between rivals Oklahoma City Thunder and the San Antonio Spurs, the NBA's Western Conference Finals will also be historic, no matter what happens on the court.

items.headline: Ranking the remaining NBA playoff teams heading into the conference finals
items.blurb: Ranking the remaining NBA playoff teams heading into the conference finals — With one more series in each conference until the NBA Finals, the four remaining teams have a clear path to a title.

items.headline: Knicks’ Anunoby practises in full again, trending towards return
items.blurb: Knicks’ Anunoby practises in full again, trending towards return — OG Anunoby is trending towards returning for Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals.

items.headline: Thunder make notable Jalen Williams announcement before WCF against Spurs
items.blurb: Thunder make notable Jalen Williams announcement before WCF against Spurs — The Oklahoma City Thunder are now fully stocked heading into their biggest postseason test.

items.headline: The 'Most NBA Game 7 points' quiz
items.blurb: The 'Most NBA Game 7 points' quiz — How many of the NBA players to score at least 100-combined points in Game 7's since the NBA/ABA merger in 1976-77 can you name in seven minutes?

items.headline: Pelicans Hiring Jamahl Mosley As Next Head Coach
items.blurb: Pelicans Hiring Jamahl Mosley As Next Head Coach — The New Orleans Pelicans are hiring Jamahl Mosley as their next head coach. New Orleans had delayed making a decision on a new coach until Mosley had parted ways with the Orlando Magic. It's been widely reported that Pelicans front office heads Joe Dumars and Troy Weaver are big fans of Mosley. Mosley is reportedly getting a five-year contract to

items.headline: Why Jamahl Mosley's Pelicans rebuild could be more challenging than Magic turnaround
items.blurb: Why Jamahl Mosley's Pelicans rebuild could be more challenging than Magic turnaround — Mosely is headed to the Pelicans just weeks after being let go by the Magic. He will likely find a more challenging situation in New Orleans.

items.headline: Dunc'd On: CLE | DET Game 7; SAS | OKC Outlook
items.blurb: Dunc'd On: CLE | DET Game 7; SAS | OKC Outlook — Cleveland turned a second-straight 7-gamer into an anti-climax, waxing the Pistons on their home floor in a game that was non-competitive. We discuss the Cavs' adjustments and how Donovan Mitchell was able to turn it from a subpar series. Also: taking stock of the Pistons' roster and what they'll need to take the next step after a disappointing (yes

items.headline: Pelicans hire Jamahl Mosley as new head coach
items.blurb: Pelicans hire Jamahl Mosley as new head coach — The former Orlando Magic head coach has agreed to a five-year contract to become the New Orleans Pelicans’ new bench boss, ESPN’s Shams Charania reported Monday.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama confident ahead of WCF clash with defending champs
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama confident ahead of WCF clash with defending champs — The Spurs are set to take on the defending champion Thunder in the Western Conference Finals.

items.headline: Spurs vs. Thunder Western Conference Finals Game 1: Watch Live Stream, NBA Playoffs on Peacock
items.blurb: Spurs vs. Thunder Western Conference Finals Game 1: Watch Live Stream, NBA Playoffs on Peacock — How to watch Spurs vs. Thunder Game 1 Tonight's Game 1 will be broadcast on NBC and is available to stream live on Peacock . Category Details Date Monday, May 18, 2026 Tip-Off Time 8:30 PM ET / 7:30 PM CT Live Stream Peacock Venue Paycom Center, Oklahoma City, OK The 2026 Western Conference Finals have

items.headline: Alex Caruso: Paces OKC in defeat
items.blurb: Alex Caruso: Paces OKC in defeat — Caruso contributed 31 points (11-19 FG, 8-14 3Pt, 1-3 FT), two rebounds, two steals, two blocks and one assist across 32 minutes in Monday's 122-115 double-overtime loss to the Spurs in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama: Makes history in victory
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama: Makes history in victory — Wembanyama racked up 41 points (14-25 FG, 1-2 3Pt, 12-13 FT), 24 rebounds, three assists, three blocks and one steal across 49 minutes in Monday's 122-115 double-overtime win over the Thunder in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Dylan Harper: Starting sans Fox
items.blurb: Dylan Harper: Starting sans Fox — Harper will start Monday's Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals against the Thunder. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: De'Aaron Fox: Ruled out Monday
items.blurb: De'Aaron Fox: Ruled out Monday — Fox (ankle) has been ruled out for Monday's Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals against the Thunder, Marc J. Spears of ESPN.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: De'Aaron Fox: Expected to play in Game 1
items.blurb: De'Aaron Fox: Expected to play in Game 1 — Head coach Mitch Johnson said he expects Fox (ankle) to play in Monday's Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals against the Thunder, Andrew Schlecht of The Athletic reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Bickerstaff says Pistons will learn from blowout loss in Game 7 against Cavaliers
items.blurb: Bickerstaff says Pistons will learn from blowout loss in Game 7 against Cavaliers — The stunning turnaround season for the Detroit Pistons ended Sunday night with a harsh reminder of how difficult the final step can be.

items.headline: Bob Myers To Explore Hiring Onsi Saleh For Sixers' President; Neil Olshey, Vince Rozman Also Candidates
items.blurb: Bob Myers To Explore Hiring Onsi Saleh For Sixers' President; Neil Olshey, Vince Rozman Also Candidates — Bob Myers will reportedly explore hiring Atlanta Hawks general manager Onsi Saleh as the next president of basketball operations for the Philadelphia 76ers, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line. The expectation is that Saleh will remain with the Hawks. The Sixers have also been linked wit

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell Says Cavs 'Can Breathe A Little Bit' Following Game 7 Win Over Pistons
items.blurb: Donovan Mitchell Says Cavs 'Can Breathe A Little Bit' Following Game 7 Win Over Pistons — The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Detroit Pistons 125-94 in Game 7 to win the Eastern Conference semifinals on Sunday night. Donovan Mitchell finished the game with 26 points, 8 assists, and 6 rebounds. Mitchell was responsible for 43 points on the night, with his teammates shooting 8-for-8 off his passes.
